description: The Bulgarian mafia refers to criminal organizations based in Bulgaria, composed primarily of ethnic Bulgarians. Emerging in the early 1990s after the fall of communism, many members originated from former wrestlers and athletes. They are involved in drug trafficking, arms smuggling, human trafficking, extortion, and other crimes. The groups have maintained close ties with politicians and have a significant presence across Europe.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Italian police, together with the Bulgarian police, dismantled a Bulgarian gang that operated mainly in the Italian region of Tuscany
country_links.context: The Bulgarian mafia operates in Italy, with a gang dismantled in Tuscany and cooperation with 'Ndrangheta.

country_links.evidence_quote: he was convicted of drug trafficking and money laundering in Bulgaria, Romania, and Italy.
country_links.context: Evelin Banev, a Bulgarian mafia figure, was convicted of drug trafficking and money laundering in Romania.

country_links.evidence_quote: Shot dead in Cape Town, South Africa in May 2023.
country_links.context: A Bulgarian mafia figure, Krasimir Kamenov, was shot dead in Cape Town, South Africa.

country_links.evidence_quote: Credit Suisse and a former employee stood trial in Switzerland ... accused of enabling a Bulgarian cocaine trafficking group to launder around CHF146 million
country_links.context: A Bulgarian cocaine trafficking group laundered money through Credit Suisse in Switzerland.

country_links.evidence_quote: Drug trafficking organizations run by Bulgarian nationals have been investigated in across US, especially in Los Angeles and Tampa.
country_links.context: Bulgarian drug trafficking organizations operate in the US, importing cocaine and ecstasy.
